title: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer’s Association with her mother in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kaity Cimo, from Scituate, will run the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer's Association in honor of her mother.
Cimo reflects on clearing her mother's kitchen, filled with memories of family meals despite her mother's Alzheimer's.
Cimo's participation in the marathon aims to honor her mother's legacy of love and nurturing through cooking.

title: On eve of trade deadline, Bruins make a horrid case for becoming buyers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins lost 6-3 to Nashville Predators, dropping to 11-14-4 on the road this season.
GM Don Sweeney contemplates trade moves as Bruins hold second Wild Card spot in Eastern Conference.
The Bruins face a challenging schedule with 12 of their final 21 games on the road.

title: Judge pauses deportation of Boston area Irish man held in custody for 6 months
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Judge David Barron pauses deportation of Seamus Culleton, an Irish man held in Texas for six months.
Culleton overstayed a 90-day visa in 2009 and has a pending green card application, with no criminal record in the U.S.
Culleton owns a small plastering business and is detained despite being married to a U.S. citizen from Boston.

title: Victim in early morning Foxborough fire identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: George Schubert, 81, and his dog died in an early morning house fire on Granite Street, Foxborough.
Foxborough, Mansfield, and Plainville firefighters responded to the scene after flames were reported early Tuesday.
The cause of the fatal fire is currently under investigation by local authorities.

title: Boston tree canopy has expanded, in step with plans for a cleaner, greener city
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's tree canopy expanded 151 acres from 2019 to 2024, now covering 28.5% of the city.
Largest tree canopy gains occurred in East Boston, Jamaica Plain, South Boston, and West Roxbury.
The expansion is part of Boston's climate goals, including the Urban Forest Plan and 2030 Climate Action Plan.

title: Kraft agrees to help cover World Cup security costs, but Foxbourough leaders still unsatisfied
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Robert Kraft's group agreed to help cover $7.8 million security costs for 2026 World Cup at Gillette Stadium.
Foxborough officials remain unsatisfied, stating the proposed security plan and funding are inadequate for the event's requirements.
The Select Board will decide on granting an event license by March 17, continuing discussions with World Cup organizers.

title: Two Saints Tavern, a familiar bar for Northeastern students, is closing after 10 years in Fenway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Saints Tavern in Fenway is closing this weekend after nearly a decade of operation, citing rising insurance costs.
The bar, a favorite among Northeastern University students, makes 85% of revenue from alcohol, impacting their insurance premiums.
Managing partner Chuck Hitchcock stated plans to close were decided after discussing selling the bar since July 2025.

title: 4 questions facing Celtics ahead of Jayson Tatum’s expected return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum is listed as 'Questionable' for the Celtics' game against the Dallas Mavericks on Friday, signaling his potential return.
The Celtics are in second place in the Eastern Conference with a 41-21 record and could benefit from Tatum's comeback.
Coach Joe Mazzulla must adjust Celtics' rotations, considering Tatum's expected minutes restriction and impact on team dynamics.

title: How did Jayson Tatum’s Achilles’ tendon recovery unfold? Recapping the Celtics star’s journey.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum expected to return from Achilles injury on Friday against the Dallas Mavericks at TD Garden.
Tatum's Achilles tendon rupture occurred in Game 4 of the 2025 Eastern Conference semifinals against the Knicks.
Celtics maintained the No. 2 seed without Tatum, who underwent surgery and extensive rehab over past 10 months.

title: Choosing which of my two cars to keep
author: John Paul
bullets: A reader seeks advice from AAA's Car Doctor on choosing between two Honda Elements, a 2007 EX and a 2005 EX.
John Paul, AAA's Car Doctor, recommends a mechanical inspection focusing on rust, oil leaks, suspension, brakes, and overall condition.
The 2007 model generally offers better safety and performance, but the 2005 may be better if it's in superior mechanical health.
